message:
feat(runtime): harden Buffer shim bounds checks and split virtual modules
into static/dynamic layers

Buffer shim (buffer_shim.rs):
- Replace `offset || 0` with `offset >>> 0` for all read/write integer
  methods to match Node.js semantics (coerce undefined/NaN to 0 via
  unsigned right-shift instead of treating 0 as falsy)
- Add explicit RangeError bounds checks on every read/write method
  (readUInt8, readUInt16LE/BE, readUInt32LE/BE, readInt8, writeUInt8,
  writeUInt16LE/BE, writeUInt32LE/BE) verifying offset + width does
  not exceed buffer length before any memory access
- Reformat methods from single-line to multi-line for readability

Virtual module architecture (extensions_js.rs):
- Split PiJsModuleState.virtual_modules into two maps:
  - static_virtual_modules: Arc<HashMap> backed by OnceLock, shared
    immutably across all runtimes (the ~100+ built-in Node.js polyfills)
  - dynamic_virtual_modules: per-runtime HashMap for repair-generated
    stubs and proxy modules
- Add default_virtual_modules_shared() using std::sync::OnceLock to
  construct the built-in map exactly once and share via Arc::clone
- Update PiJsResolver and PiJsLoader to consult dynamic before static,
  ensuring runtime-generated repairs take precedence over built-ins
- Update compile_module_source() to accept both maps with dynamic-first
  lookup semantics
- Remove the Intl polyfill (Collator, NumberFormat, DateTimeFormat) —
  QuickJS now provides native Intl support, making the minimal polyfill
  redundant and potentially masking the real implementation
- Update compile_module_source test call sites to pass two HashMap args

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
